reason: Both reach the same correct, well-reasoned conclusion (don't fight a price war, segment/differentiate, expect the cut to be unsustainable, use targeted retention offers) with nearly identical structure. B edges ahead on concrete, actionable specifics: quantified churn/win-rate thresholds for when to revisit (>2-3%/mo churn, win rate drop >20pts) versus A's vaguer 'watch for 1-2 quarters'; an explicit standalone tactic (cheaper/unbundled SKU) rather than folding it only into an exception clause; and a genuinely new suggestion to consider raising capital now from a position of strength. These are actionable additions, not padding, since they'd change what the asker measures and considers doing next. A is slightly cleaner prose but doesn't add anything B lacks.
